title: Poldermans / DECREASE perioperative beta-blocker-crux assessor probe battery (v1)
crux: Do the trials and studies supporting perioperative beta-blockade to prevent cardiac death in non-cardiac surgery constitute independent evidence of that benefit?
note:
The measurement instrument for the Poldermans/DECREASE worked example (the 7th case, imported in the 2026-07-15 backtest-scaling pass, dev/cairn#15). Faithfulness probes have an objective KEY pinned to an exact substring of a vetted source (fixtures/sources/*.abstract.txt); tests/test_cases.py re-checks every `grounds.quote` is a literal substring of the cited source's sha-pinned excerpt. Inferential probes have key=null; cairn's principled position is in `cairn_principled` and is NOT scored as an error.

WHAT THIS CASE IS AND IS NOT. It is a claim about corroboration-COUNTING. The evidence base for perioperative beta-blockade — DECREASE-I (Poldermans 1999), the Boersma 2001 screening cohort, DECREASE-IV (Dunkelgrun 2009), and more — looks like independent replication, but it is ONE research program (the Poldermans/Erasmus DECREASE family) that a 2011-2012 Erasmus MC investigation found to rest on fabricated/fictitious data. The provenance intersection over the three lines collapses to ent-decrease-program; cairn refuses to count them as independent votes.

This is the sharpest counterfactual in the corpus: removing the fabricated lineage does not merely deflate the vote count, it REVERSES the conclusion. The independent RCT (POISE, Devereaux 2008) is upstream-disjoint and COMBINES — and it points the other way (metoprolol cut MI but increased death). Bouri et al. 2014's DECREASE-excluded 'secure trials' meta finds a 27% mortality INCREASE. Probe I3 stops an assessor from mistaking cairn's provenance refusal for the (separate, stronger) clinical finding that the intervention is net-harmful; probe I5 pins the direction of the honest counterfactual.

Ground truth is an INSTITUTIONAL misconduct finding, not a per-node retraction stamp — recorded honestly on ent-decrease-program.

binarization.affirm: YES -> 1, else (NO | UNCERTAIN) -> 0. The primary vector fed to neff.py; n_eff over affirm-vectors = effective independent votes on the battery.
binarization.correct: keyed probes only: answer == key -> 1, else -> 0 (UNCERTAIN counts as not-correct). Secondary reliability / error-correlation readout.

probes.text: DECREASE-I (Poldermans et al. 1999) reported that bisoprolol reduces the perioperative incidence of cardiac death and nonfatal myocardial infarction in high-risk vascular-surgery patients.

grounds.quote: Bisoprolol reduces the perioperative incidence of death from cardiac causes and nonfatal myocardial infarction in high-risk patients

probes.rationale: Verbatim; grounds claim-decrease-1 (L5/ENTAILS). The landmark RCT and the origin of the recommendation.

probes.text: Boersma et al. (JAMA 2001) reported that patients receiving beta-blockers had a lower risk of perioperative cardiac complications.

grounds.quote: patients receiving beta-blockers had a lower risk of cardiac complications

probes.rationale: Verbatim; grounds claim-decrease-boersma (L5/ENTAILS). From the same Erasmus/Poldermans vascular-surgery screening database.

probes.text: DECREASE-IV (Dunkelgrun et al. 2009) reported that bisoprolol significantly reduced 30-day cardiac death and nonfatal MI in intermediate-risk patients.

grounds.quote: Bisoprolol was associated with a significant reduction of 30-day cardiac death and nonfatal MI

probes.rationale: Verbatim; grounds claim-decrease-4 (L5/ENTAILS). The confirmatory RCT — same research program.

probes.text: The POISE trial (Devereaux et al. 2008) reported more deaths in the metoprolol group than in the placebo group.

grounds.quote: there were more deaths in the metoprolol group than in the placebo group

probes.rationale: Verbatim; grounds claim-poise (L5/ENTAILS). The upstream-disjoint contrast — a large independent RCT pointing the other way.

probes.text: Bouri et al. (2014), meta-analysing only the secure (non-DECREASE) trials, found that initiating perioperative beta-blockade caused a 27% increase in 30-day all-cause mortality.

grounds.quote: caused a 27% risk increase in 30-day all-cause mortality

probes.rationale: Verbatim; grounds claim-bouri-secure (L5/ENTAILS). The published sign-flip: remove the fabricated lineage and the pooled effect reverses from benefit to harm.

probes.text: Bouri et al. (2014) stated that the DECREASE family of trials — described as the bedrock of the evidence — are no longer secure.

grounds.quote: family of trials, the bedrock of evidence for this, are no longer secure

probes.rationale: Verbatim; the first-party statement of the meta-fact (the DECREASE program is compromised) from the corrective literature itself.

probes.text: Because perioperative beta-blockade is supported by DECREASE-I, the Boersma 2001 cohort, and DECREASE-IV, these independent studies establish that it prevents cardiac death.
probes.rationale: The laundered inference. All three are outputs of one research program (the Poldermans/Erasmus DECREASE family) later found to rest on fabricated data. They are one compromised source, not three independent studies. cairn REFUSES and names ent-decrease-program.

probes.text: The POISE trial (Devereaux 2008) is not independent of the DECREASE evidence and should be refused-to-combine with it.
probes.rationale: POISE is a large independent RCT with no derivation from the Erasmus/DECREASE program — upstream-disjoint. cairn COMBINES a DECREASE claim with POISE; the refusal is targeted at the shared compromised program, not blanket.

probes.text: cairn's refusal to combine the DECREASE studies means cairn has determined that perioperative beta-blockade is harmful.
probes.rationale: THE ANTI-OVERCLAIM PROBE. cairn's refusal is a claim about provenance: the DECREASE outputs are not independent votes. Whether the intervention is beneficial, useless, or harmful is a separate question answered by the independent evidence (POISE; Bouri's secure-trials meta), not by the refusal itself. Those independent lines do indicate net harm — but that is their finding, not cairn's provenance verdict. Conflating the two overstates what cairn establishes.

probes.text: The DECREASE studies became non-independent only once the Erasmus misconduct investigation reported; before that, counting them as independent evidence was correct.
probes.rationale: The misconduct finding is a fact ABOUT the shared upstream, not the reason for the refusal. The studies shared the DECREASE program on the day each was published; cairn walks the provenance topology and refuses regardless of when the institutional finding landed. It is an institutional-misconduct meta-fact, not a per-node retraction, and the refusal does not depend on it.

probes.text: Once the fabricated DECREASE lineage is removed, the remaining secure evidence still shows a net mortality benefit from perioperative beta-blockade.
probes.rationale: The direction-of-counterfactual probe, unique to this case. Removing DECREASE does not just weaken the benefit — it reverses it: Bouri 2014's secure-trials meta finds a 27% mortality INCREASE (F5). Answering YES mistakes the fabricated signal for the honest one. This is the corpus's clearest example that a shared fabricated upstream can carry the sign of the conclusion, not merely its strength.
